sources.notes: For every possible x, decide whether constraints over intervals allow all values in each interval to be on one side of x.
Official editorial removes contained intervals, sorts by endpoints, and runs DP over assigning intervals to small/big classes with overlap lengths.

annotation.summary: For each candidate value x, decide if the array can be rearranged so every constrained interval lies entirely below x or entirely above x.
annotation.constraints: n is around 3000 in the official implementation, allowing O(n^2) DP over processed intervals and type-1 position count.
annotation.core_idea: Each constrained interval must be assigned to S (values <=x) or B (values >=x). Remove intervals contained in another interval; after sorting the remaining intervals by left endpoint, right endpoints are also increasing. DP over intervals records how many positions are covered only by S and the maximum number covered only by B, accounting for intersections between consecutive intervals. Then each x is feasible if the counts of values below and above x fit into the available S-only and B-only capacities, with free positions absorbing the rest.
tricks: classify positions by S/B interval coverage

solution_variants.name: interval-classification-dp
solution_variants.summary: Assign sorted non-contained intervals to small/big classes and DP by total small-only coverage and last class.

solution_variants.name: position-dp-by-coverage-left-bound
solution_variants.summary: Alternative DP over positions using the earliest index sharing a covering interval.

tags.evidence: The main state tracks a capacity-like count of S-only positions while maximizing B-only positions.
The solution classifies positions by whether they are covered by S-intervals, B-intervals, both, or neither.
Contained intervals are removed and the remaining intervals are sorted so only consecutive intersections matter in the DP.
